title: StatefulSets, DaemonSets, Jobs, CronJobs, and Controller Selection · Control-Plane Trace: K8s Daemonset Node Coverage
summary: A data intake system has interchangeable APIs, identity-bound workers, node agents, finite migrations, and scheduled reconciliation tasks requiring different controllers. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s daemonset node coverage increment in controller selection and completion contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use DaemonSets for node-local facilities with selectors, tolerations, priority, rolling updates, resource bounds, and control-plane coverage decisions.
Design StatefulSet ordinal identity, headless discovery, stable claims, ordering, update partition, scaling, and failure recovery around application guarantees.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Daemonset Node Coverage · Theory K8s Daemonset Node Coverage
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory platform runs a durable database as a Deployment, a node log agent as one replica, and a finite migration as a long-running Pod. content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example platform runs a durable database as a Deployment, a node log agent as one replica, and a finite migration as a long-running Pod. Choose Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet, Job, or CronJob from identity, placement, completion, concurrency, and retry contracts. Competency probe: Use DaemonSets for node-local facilities with selectors, tolerations, priority, rolling updates, resource bounds, and control-plane coverage decisions. A defensible solution applies K8s Daemonset Node Coverage, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to controller selection and completion contract.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-workload-controller-selection-2
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-workload-controller-selection-2
    artifact: controller-selection.yaml
    decision: worked k8s workload controller selection 2
    environment: rootless development cluster with an empty namespace
    fault-injection: make an admission dependency unavailable during an upgrade
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      stateless-api: Deployment
      identity-bound-worker: StatefulSet
      node-agent: DaemonSet
      one-time-migration: Job
      scheduled-reconcile: CronJob
      selection-proof: identity-completion-placement-update
    verify: kubectl get deploy,sts,ds,job,cronjob -n intake -o json
    failure: Reject Deployment-for-everything, exactly-once Job claims, CronJob clock certainty, or StatefulSet as automatic data replication.
    repair: Select from identity, completion, placement, update, storage, retry, idempotency, and failure evidence; then test controller-specific recovery.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A DaemonSet always runs exactly one Pod on every node regardless of eligibility or updates. This fails the K8s Daemonset Node Coverage contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
